beats.reasoning: Routine commute from home to his workplace in Redmond.

beats.activity: Works as an archivist: cataloging, handling boxes from recent donations, and updating finding aids.
beats.reasoning: Morning focus on meticulous cataloging that fits his archivist routine.

beats.reasoning: Stays on task through the afternoon, then heads out once work wraps.

Ends the day at home with a full overnight sleep block.

diary: Woke up at 6:30 with that bright, early-June optimism—coffee in hand, weather check, and a quick glance at the USA vs Wales kickoff at 11. Everything felt orderly, like the day had a plan. I drove into Overlake and got into the archive groove right away: cataloging new donations, sliding boxes into their proper spots, updating finding aids like I was tightening a belt on the past. Around lunchtime I grabbed food from Whole Foods Market and actually ate outside—sun on my face, low stakes, just me and the soft hum of the day. The light rail shutdown between Westlake and University District made me stick to driving and detouring a bit, but it wasn’t stressful. After work I wandered into Easy Street Records on Capitol Hill and picked up a new vinyl. The Kraken Tavern afterward with my friend felt like a reset button, and later we played board games at Redmond Town Center—laughs, easy pacing. I ended the night back at 8855 Avondale Rd NE, records spinning, ferry schedule checked for fun. I felt quietly content, like everything slotted into place.
thought_process: I tried to structure the day around momentum: morning routine first, then steady archive intake while my brain was sharp. Overlake is where I’m most “me,” so I focused on cataloging and finding aids before any errands could scatter my attention. The light rail closure nudged me toward a more car-based route, and it also probably made me appreciate how my errands were clustered—Fred Meyer for supplies, then Capitol Hill for the vinyl, so I didn’t waste energy backtracking. World events definitely colored the air: the USA vs Wales match made downtown feel a little more alive, and even though I wasn’t watching yet, I moved with that anticipatory buzz. My mood shifted from calm productivity to playful nostalgia when the record found its way into my hands, and it stayed warm through board games. By evening, I felt grounded—tired, but in a good way.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
